name: Enterprise Service Bus (Logical)
strategicIntent: Define a logical enterprise service bus layer for complex multi-system orchestrations, canonical data model enforcement, and protocol mediation across McCain's ERP, CRM, supply chain, and manufacturing ecosystems.
problemStatement: McCain's enterprise landscape includes 200+ applications across SAP, Salesforce, factory MES systems, and logistics platforms. Without a logical integration backbone, each point-to-point integration creates exponential complexity, inconsistent data formats, and ungoverned data flows.

useCases.use: Multi-step order-to-cash orchestrations spanning SAP, Salesforce, and logistics
useCases.dontUse: Simple two-system REST API integrations that don't need orchestration

useCases.use: Canonical data model transformations between ERP and CRM systems
useCases.dontUse: Real-time event streaming where ESB orchestration adds unnecessary latency

useCases.use: Protocol mediation between SOAP-based legacy systems and REST-based cloud services
useCases.dontUse: Intra-cluster microservice communication handled by service mesh

components: Azure Integration Services — Logic Apps, Service Bus, API Management, Event Grid
Azure Logic Apps — orchestration workflows with enterprise connectors
Azure Service Bus — reliable messaging backbone with topics and sessions
Canonical Data Model library — standardized schemas for order, product, customer, plant entities
Azure Data Factory — batch integration and data movement pipelines
Azure Monitor — integration flow monitoring, SLA tracking, and alerting
interactionSteps: Source system publishes business event or sends message to ESB ingress (Service Bus topic)
Logic App orchestrator picks up message and applies canonical data model transformation
Orchestrator routes transformed message to target system(s) based on content-based routing rules
Protocol mediation layer converts between SOAP/REST/file-based formats as needed
Target system confirms receipt; orchestrator updates flow state and audit log
Failed deliveries are routed to DLQ with alerting for integration operations team
Azure Monitor tracks end-to-end flow latency, success rates, and SLA compliance
securityConsiderations: All ESB message payloads must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Integration flows handling PII must comply with data residency requirements per region
Service Bus namespaces must use managed identity authentication, not shared access keys
Logic App connectors to external systems must store credentials in Key Vault
Audit logs for all integration flows must be retained for compliance (minimum 1 year)

guardrails.rule: All multi-system orchestrations must use the logical ESB layer, not point-to-point
Canonical data model must be used for all cross-system entity transformations
All integration flows must be registered in the integration catalog
All integration flows must have DLQ handling and alerting configured
PII-bearing flows must comply with regional data residency requirements

exceptionProcess: Submit Integration Architecture Board request for point-to-point integrations bypassing the ESB layer. Must include integration complexity analysis and governance plan for ongoing maintenance.

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Initial logical ESB pattern with Azure Integration Services

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Added canonical data model library and content-based routing

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Added data residency guardrails and integration catalog governance
